name: Los Canchales Reservoir Circular Trail
description: Embalse de Los Canchales circular is an easy 2.7-mile (4.4 km) jogging route through dehesas and scrubland, perfect for birdwatching.
region_intro: Jogging around Embalse de Los Canchales offers diverse natural features, making it an excellent destination for outdoor activities. The area is characterized by a perimeter dirt track that encircles the reservoir, providing routes with minimal elevation changes. Surrounding landscapes include characteristic Iberian dehesas and scrubland, offering picturesque scenery and a tranquil environment. The reservoir itself features shallow waters, numerous islets, and abundant aquatic vegetation, contributing to a rich and well-preserved ecosystem. Best jogging routes around Embalse de Los Canchales The most popular jogging route is Los Canchales Reservoir Circular Trail, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes about 32 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the reservoir's perimeter, offering views of the natural surroundings and local birdlife. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from La Garrovilla, a moderate 4.0 miles (6.4 km) path. This route leads through dehesas and scrubland, providing a tranquil setting for a run of about 40 minutes. Local runners also love the La Nava de Santiago Circular Route, an 8.6 miles (13.9 km) trail leading through characteristic Iberian dehesas and scrubland, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Jogging around Embalse de Los Canchales is defined by its perimeter dirt tracks, characteristic dehesas, and tranquil scrubland. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more moderate running routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 runners have used komoot to explore Embalse de Los Canchales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Embalse de Los Canchales?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around Embalse de Los Canchales, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and a few more challenging options to explore the area's natural beauty.

Q: Are there easy running trails suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, Embalse de Los Canchales offers several easy running trails. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280636816/los-canchales-reservoir-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Canchales Reservoir Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.4 km long and features minimal elevation changes, making it perfect for a gentle run while enjoying the reservoir views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes?
A: The jogging routes primarily follow a perimeter dirt track around the reservoir, known for being 'very rodable' or easy to traverse. You'll also run through characteristic Iberian dehesas (wooded pastures) and scrubland, offering a mix of open views and shaded sections under holm oaks.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for advanced runners?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282231583/la-nava-de-santiago-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Nava de Santiago Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.9 km trail that leads through extensive dehesas and scrubland. While not classified as 'difficult', its length provides a good endurance test.

Q: Can I spot wildlife while running around the reservoir?
A: Absolutely! Embalse de Los Canchales is a protected area, designated as a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA). You can expect to see a diverse range of birdlife, including cranes and geese in winter, and osprey, whiskered terns, and common pratincoles in summer. Year-round residents like common spoonbills and marsh harriers are also frequently observed.

Q: Are there any circular running routes?
A: Yes, many of the routes are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280636816/los-canchales-reservoir-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Canchales Reservoir Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a complete loop around a section of the reservoir. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from La Garrovilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Embalse de Los Canchales suitable for family jogging?
A: Yes, the area is generally very family-friendly. The easy, flat dirt tracks, especially around the reservoir's perimeter, are suitable for families with children who enjoy running or walking. The natural surroundings and birdwatching opportunities add to the experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Embalse de Los Canchales?
A: The reservoir is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for running. Winter is also a great time, especially for birdwatching, with cranes and geese present. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late evenings are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or interesting places to explore along the running paths?
A: While running, you'll encounter picturesque views of the shallow waters, numerous islets, and abundant aquatic vegetation of the reservoir. The surrounding dehesas with their holm oaks also provide scenic stretches. There's an interpretation center near the dam that offers more information about the local ecosystem, which can be a point of interest before or after your run.

Q: Is there parking available near the running trails?
A: Yes, there are typically parking areas accessible near the main entry points to the reservoir and its trails, making it convenient for visitors arriving by car. Specific parking details might vary depending on your chosen starting point.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: Embalse de Los Canchales is a natural protected area. While many trails in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always best to check local regulations regarding pets, especially in areas with sensitive wildlife. If allowed, dogs should generally be kept on a leash to protect the local fauna and flora.

Q: What makes the running experience unique at Embalse de Los Canchales?
A: The unique combination of a well-maintained, 'rodable' dirt track, minimal elevation changes, and the stunning natural backdrop of a protected wetland makes running here special. You're not just exercising; you're immersed in a tranquil environment rich with diverse birdlife and characteristic Iberian landscapes of dehesas and scrubland.
tips: Dam that collects the waters of the Riber de Lácara and where it is located to the north of the municipality of La Garrovilla.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog the Embalse de Los Canchales — circular trail?
faqs.answer: The circular jogging route is approximately 4.4 kilometers long and typically takes around 30-35 minutes to complete at a steady jogging pace. It's an easy route suitable for all fitness levels.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The 4.4 km circular route is predominantly paved, offering an easy and smooth surface for jogging. While this specific route is paved, the wider area around the reservoir also features longer dirt tracks and cycling routes that are also very runnable due to minimal elevation changes.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Los Canchales — circular trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely. This trail is classified as easy with an elevation gain of only about 30 meters over 4.4 kilometers, making it ideal for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: The area is a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA), so it's excellent for birdwatching. You might spot cranes and geese in winter, or osprey, whiskered tern, and collared pratincole in summer. The surrounding dehesas also host steppe birds. The Los Canchales Reservoir itself, with its shallow waters and islets, is a central feature attracting diverse species.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit for jogging and wildlife viewing?
faqs.answer: The trail offers good jogging conditions and birdwatching opportunities year-round. Winter is excellent for seeing cranes and geese, while summer brings species like osprey and terns. The clear skies also make it suitable for astrotourism, especially outside of peak daylight hours.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Embalse de Los Canchales — circular trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations aren't detailed, the trail is easily accessible. Look for designated parking areas near the reservoir, particularly around the main access points to the circular routes. Public transport options to nearby La Garrovilla might also be available, requiring a short connection to the reservoir.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails in natural areas, but it's always best to keep them on a leash to protect the local wildlife, especially since this is a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA). Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Embalse de Los Canchales ZEPA?
faqs.answer: As a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA), the Embalse de Los Canchales is a protected natural space. Typically, there are no entrance fees or permits required for general access to enjoy the trails. However, always respect conservation efforts and follow any posted regulations regarding wildlife and habitat protection.

faqs.question: Are there any longer jogging options if I want to extend my run?
faqs.answer: Yes, beyond the 4.4 km circular route, there's a perimeter dirt track exceeding 23 km around the reservoir. Additionally, a 43 km cycling route around the reservoir is described as 'very runnable' due to its minimal elevation changes, offering a low-to-medium difficulty option for longer jogs.

faqs.question: Can I find food or accommodation near the Embalse de Los Canchales?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is focused on nature, you can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in nearby towns such as La Garrovilla. These services are typically a short drive from the reservoir.

faqs.question: Are there any specific viewpoints or landmarks along the circular route?
faqs.answer: The primary landmark is the Los Canchales Reservoir itself, which offers scenic views of the water, islets, and diverse birdlife. The trail provides continuous views of this central feature, surrounded by dehesas and scrubland.
